publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 192-194. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: [word partially cut off] gentleman in Town, could only lead to disappointment; at least with any Parliamentary gentleman certainly!-- And I am very glad I have not attempted such an experiment; for I think failure would have vexed me.
But such plans might come in well sometimes perhaps for the Country , where the misfortune to me is always a want & lack of any peculiar excitements.
We have a dinner- -card from the old Philips's for Weddy, 16th March.
I have accepted; but begging permission to take Hester, if you should be out of Town on that evening.--
I have given orders about the Minutes; as you desired.--
This morning Hester & I have been with Wheatstone to the Government Economical-
-Geological-Mining Museum. It is most interesting; & you must certainly see it. It is much in your way.
I made acquaintance there with De La Biche, who was very obliging & polite, in showing & explaining many things.
I suppose that your Uncle's Miss Neville is a cousin of Mrs Locke's; a clear reason against our having anything to do with her
I think! And now add your Uncle has not written you a line on the subject. It appears to me marked;-- his not doing so;-- and very confirmatory of the lady being one of the Mrs Locke click & tribe.
I believe the Hen wrote to you of my going down with her last evening. I am particularly glad that

I was with her. For she was (tho' she will not admit or own it), annoyed & nervous at our accident on the Railway, (of which I suppose she has told you); & my own composure & absence of all fear were a great comfort to her I know.
The occurence was I conceive the result of gross neglect; & it certainly was not altogether agreeable to have the

Fast-Train upon our heels as we had the whole way afterwards. We were bundled out at Esher in a desperate hurry, as they scarcly dared stop; & we watched the two Trains afterwards for some way, running a kind of fearful race with each other.--
